message.content: reaquaint

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
19	
20	_No cycle currently in progress._
21	
22	## Todo
23	
24	 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
25	 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
26	 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
27	 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
28	
29	 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so
30	 you can grep, count, and reference them ("let's work on
31	 #3"). You don't hand-maintain the numbers — insert, delete,
32	 or reorder entries freely, then `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run`
33	 renumbers the list and normalizes continuation-line indent.
34	 `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo`
35	 is the read-only check.
36	
37	1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
38	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
39	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
40	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
41	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
42	   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
43	   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
44	   (`init.rs`, tests).
45	
46	   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
47	   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
48	   issue.
49	2. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
50	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
51	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
52	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
53	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
54	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
55	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
56	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
57	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
58	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
59	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
60	3. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
61	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
62	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
63	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
64	   the felt bottleneck.
65	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
66	4. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
67	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
68	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
69	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
70	   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
71	   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
72	5. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
73	   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
74	   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
75	   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
76	   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
77	   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
78	   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
79	   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
80	6. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
81	   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
82	   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
83	   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
84	   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
85	   questions in the stub. [[4]]
86	7. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
87	   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
88	   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
89	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
90	   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
91	   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
92	   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
93	   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
94	   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
95	   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
96	   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
97	8. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
98	   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
99	   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
100	   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
101	   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
102	   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
103	   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
104	   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
105	   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
106	9. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
107	   Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
108	   → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
109	   signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
110	   communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
111	10. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
112	    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
113	    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
114	    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
115	    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
116	    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
117	    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
118	11. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
119	    `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
120	    the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
121	    across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
122	    coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
123	12. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
124	13. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
125	    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
126	    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
127	    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
128	    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
129	    or drop one spelling. [[10]]
130	14. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
131	    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
132	    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
133	    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[11]]
134	15. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
135	    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
136	    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
137	    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
138	    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
139	    [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
140	16. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
141	    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
142	    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
143	    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
144	    close-out). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
145	17. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
146	    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
147	    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
148	    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
149	    default). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
150	18. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
151	    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
152	    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
153	    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
154	    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
155	    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
156	    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
157	19. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
158	    once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
159	    override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
160	    init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
161	    post-init commands can. Depends on the
162	    `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
163	    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
164	20. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
165	    `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
166	    `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
167	    `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
168	    per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
169	    `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
170	    visual inconsistency.
171	21. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
172	    `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
173	    `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
174	    under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
175	    Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
176	22. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
177	    section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
178	    Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
179	    Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
180	    Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
181	    names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
182	    in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
183	    broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
184	    sweep.
185	23. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
186	    repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
187	    natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
188	    it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[17]].
189	24. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
190	    `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
191	    before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
192	    2026-04-24).
193	25. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
194	    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
195	    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
196	    place first. [[12]]
197	26. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
198	    message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
199	    Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[18]]
200	27. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
201	    through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
202	    0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
203	28. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
204	    `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
205	    integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
206	    plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
207	    resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
208	    matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
209	    per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
210	    Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
211	    `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[19]]
212	29. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
213	    (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
214	    fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[17]]
215	30. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
216	    only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
217	    differs from entry. [[20]]
218	31. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
219	    anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
220	    pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[12]]
221	32. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
222	    `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
223	    that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
224	    both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
225	    needed for the new layout.
226	33. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
227	    adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
228	    code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
229	    existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
230	    src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[21]]
231	34. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[22]]
232	35. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[23]]
233	36. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[24]]
234	37. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
235	38. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
236	39. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
237	40. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[25]],[[26]]
238	41. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[27]]
239	42. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[28]],[[29]]
240	43. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
241	    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
242	    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
243	    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
244	    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
245	    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
246	    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
247	    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
248	    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
249	    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
250	    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
251	    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
252	    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
253	    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
254	    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
255	    it adds value over the resolution chain.
256	44. **`validate-todo` / `fix-todo`: flag malformed lines.**
257	    A column-0 line inside `## Todo` / `## Bugs` that is
258	    neither an entry (`N. `) nor a heading is malformed;
259	    it's currently tolerated silently. Report it so stray
260	    lines / typos surface.
261	
262	## Bugs
263	
264	   Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix
265	   for. Each entry should describe what goes wrong, when,
266	   and the cost of the failure. Numbering convention same as
267	   `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …).
268	
269	1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
270	   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
271	   four gaps:
272	   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
273	     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
274	     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
275	     are never surfaced.
276	   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
277	     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
278	     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
279	     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
280	     printed twice.
281	   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
282	     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
283	     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
284	     on write would close this.
285	   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
286	     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
287	     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
288	     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
289	     where there may be no next run.
290	
291	   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
292	   patches one related case — the detached child eating
293	   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
294	   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
295	   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
296	   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.
297	
298	## Done
299	
300	Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
301	and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.
302	
303	_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._
304	
305	- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[1]]
306	- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]
307	- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[3]]
308	- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[30]]
309	- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[31]]
310	- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[32]]
311

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

_No cycle currently in progress._

## Todo

 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so
 you can grep, count, and reference them ("let's work on
 #3"). You don't hand-maintain the numbers — insert, delete,
 or reorder entries freely, then `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run`
 renumbers the list and normalizes continuation-line indent.
 `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo`
 is the read-only check.

1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
2. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
3. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
   the felt bottleneck.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
4. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
5. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
6. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
   questions in the stub. [[4]]
7. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
8. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
9. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
   Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
   → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
   signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
   communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
10. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
11. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
    `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
    the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
    across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
    coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
12. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
13. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
    or drop one spelling. [[10]]
14. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with
    `#[allow(dead_code)]`. Either wire the
    skip-preflight-on-resume behavior or drop the flag. [[11]]
15. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Was 0.42.0-4 (deferred when cycle pivoted to icr
    rebase work; cycle closed at -4.7). State machine
    becomes scope-aware — single-side scope skips
    `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/`finalize-claude`.
    [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
16. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;
    topology (`--por` vs dual) is the separate `--por`
    boolean. Was 0.42.0-6 (deferred at -4.7
    close-out). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
17. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
    `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
    as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
    bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
    default). [[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
18. Unify `.vc-config.toml` accessors onto Pattern B
    (typed struct + `load_from(path)`, like new
    `config::UserConfig` and `push::resolve_state_layout`).
    Replaces the map-typed helpers in `desc_helpers.rs` /
    `fix_desc.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` with a typed
    `WorkspaceConfig` struct. ~50 LOC, mechanical.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
19. Layered config precedence (user → workspace → CLI)
    once `WorkspaceConfig` is typed. Workspace can
    override `[github].owner` etc. for a specific project;
    init can't use the layer (chicken-and-egg) but
    post-init commands can. Depends on the
    `WorkspaceConfig` typed-struct refactor above.
    Candidate for 0.41.2. [[16]]
20. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
    `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
    `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
    `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
    per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
    `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
    visual inconsistency.
21. Replace "Step N" log prefixes with single-word
    `label: body` convention (`bookmark`, `provision`,
    `colocate`, `cross-ref`, `symlink`, …); indent labels
    under per-side `code:` / `bot:` headers in dual.
    Originally planned as 0.41.1-6.7; deferred.
22. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
    section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
    Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
    Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
    Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
    names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
    in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
    broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
    sweep.
23. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
    repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
    natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
    it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [[17]].
24. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
    `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
    before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
    2026-04-24).
25. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
    place first. [[12]]
26. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
    message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
    Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [[18]]
27. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
    through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
    0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
28. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
    `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
    integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
    plus chores↔commit consistency — every `[N]:` anchor
    resolves, and each chores `##` section's recorded title
    matches its `Commits:` commit's title — and reports
    per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of failed checks.
    Implementation: promote `verify_state_sanity` /
    `verify_completion_sanity` from push.rs to `common.rs`. [[19]]
29. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
    (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
    fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [[17]]
30. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
    only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
    differs from entry. [[20]]
31. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
    anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
    pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [[12]]
32. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
    `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
    that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
    both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
    needed for the new layout.
33. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
    adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
    code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
    existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
    src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [[21]]
34. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [[22]]
35. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [[23]]
36. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [[24]]
37. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
38. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
39. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
40. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [[25]],[[26]]
41. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [[27]]
42. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [[28]],[[29]]
43. Test-tempdir override resolution chain. Both
    `src/test_helpers::unique_base` and
    `tests/common/unique_base` currently use
    `std::env::temp_dir()` (= `$TMPDIR`). Generalize to
    resolve in priority order: explicit env var (e.g.
    `VC_X1_TEST_TMPDIR`) → user config
    (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`) → local
    `.vc-config.toml` → `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback.
    Useful when a developer wants tests on a tmpfs / SSD /
    project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
    Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
    (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
    easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
    env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
    `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
    it adds value over the resolution chain.
44. **`validate-todo` / `fix-todo`: flag malformed lines.**
    A column-0 line inside `## Todo` / `## Bugs` that is
    neither an entry (`N. `) nor a heading is malformed;
    it's currently tolerated silently. Report it so stray
    lines / typos surface.

## Bugs

   Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix
   for. Each entry should describe what goes wrong, when,
   and the cost of the failure. Numbering convention same as
   `## Todo` (manual `1.` `2.` …).

1. **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` is racy and
   bounded by "next vc-x1 run".** The current model has
   four gaps:
   - **Stale forever.** Markers sit on disk until the
     next `vc-x1 <anything>` runs. If the user abandons
     the workspace (e.g., CI / scheduled use), failures
     are never surfaced.
   - **Concurrent surface_previous_failures.** Two
     `vc-x1` runs racing: both `read_dir`, both print
     the marker, one deletes (the other's `remove_file`
     silently fails). The user sees the same failure
     printed twice.
   - **Mid-write torn read.** A `finalize --exec` child
     writing a marker while a sibling `vc-x1` is
     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
     on write would close this.
   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
     where there may be no next run.

   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
   patches one related case — the detached child eating
   its own prior markers before the user can see them —
   but doesn't address any of the above. Holistic fix
   needs locking + atomic writes + maybe a
   notify-at-failure path for hands-off use.

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-05-15 (0.44.0–0.50.0 batch)._

- chores subdir reshape — `notes/chores-*.md` → `notes/chores/`; 0.44.0–0.50.0 Done batch migrated to done.md (0.51.0) [[1]]
- `sb_ide` elimination — banner off by default (`-V` toggles), `bm_track` → `debug!`, `sb_ide` + `SubcommandRunner::{is_detached_exec, suppress_banner}` removed (0.52.0) [[2]]
- todo renumber + `notes/fix-todo.py` interim script; cycle re-scoped at close-out, scope CLI cleanup deferred to 0.54.0 (0.53.0) [[3]]
- scope CLI cleanup — `--scope` roles-only, `--por` boolean replaces `ScopeKind`, `Scope` relocated to `options_flags/`, sync gains `-R` (0.54.0) [[30]]
- validate-todo / fix-todo subcommands — check + renumber `## Todo` / `## Bugs` entry numbering, replacing `notes/fix-todo.py` (0.55.0) [[31]]
- refine cycle protocol — one protocol (Preparation/Work-N/Close-out), `.`-separator nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Preparation, push & squash discretionary, `.claude` once per push, two-gate review (work then message, both before commit), CLAUDE.md cycle/commit/push docs consolidated into one linear `## Cycle Protocol` (~39% smaller) (0.56.0) [[32]]

Reacquainted. Quick state:

- **Branch:** clean, no cycle in progress. Last commit `25dfcb8 docs: refine cycle protocol (0.56.0)`.
- **`.claude` repo:** has the usual in-session changes; nothing committed since `0.56.0`.
- **Recent arc:** `0.56.0` consolidated the Cycle Protocol docs (one protocol — Preparation/Work-N/Close-out, dotted nested numbering with trailing-`0`=Prep, push/squash discretionary, two-gate review). `0.55.0` shipped `validate-todo` / `fix-todo`. `0.54.0` did the scope CLI cleanup.
- **Top of `## Todo`:** (1) single-field `options_flags` leaves → rename inner field to `value`, sweep `repo`/`dry_run`/`private`/`account`/`config`/`use_template` + consumers. (2)–(3) `linkme` / `inventory` investigations. Lots more behind.

Ready when you are.

message.content: So the 0.56.0 was a "Merge (non-FF) commit" so the history between it and 0.55.0 has a trapezoidal shape with 0.55.0 having 2 children (0.56.0 and 0.56.0-0) and 0.56.0 has 2 parents (0.55.0 and 0.56.0-3). We need to teach `vc-x1 push` to handle that situation. Create a todo for it.

file.content:
Workflow` → `## Cycle Protocol`; rewrite the review
  sections (provisionally to commit-first; corrected at
  `-2`); add `notes/forks-multi-user.md` "Staged fork
  integration" subsection.
- 0.56.0-2 Work — the big-lever: collapse 8 sections into
  one linear `## Cycle Protocol`; the two-gate
  review-before-commit model lands here. (`Cargo.toml`
  bump to `-2` backfilled at `-3` via `jj edit`.)
- 0.56.0-3 Work + close-out bookkeeping — move
  squash-or-keep text from `### Close-out` to `### Pushing`;
  add **Merge (non-FF)** as a third close-out shape; clear
  `## In Progress`, add `## Done` one-liner, finalize this
  chores section.
- 0.56.0 Close-out (bare, non-FF merge) — merge commit
  with parents (`0.55.0`, `-3`); `Cargo.toml` `0.56.0-3` →
  `0.56.0` (the merge's only content beyond the merge
  itself).

### Outcome

A bigger cycle than the original pickup framed. Mid-cycle
re-scoping (`-2` rewriting `-1`'s sections from scratch)
and a review-model correction (two gates, before commit,
instead of commit-first) drove the consolidation. Final
CLAUDE.md reads as one linear protocol section instead of
an eight-section assembly job.

Landed via the new **non-FF merge** ("trapezoid") shape —
dogfooded for the first time this cycle. The merge commit
at bare `0.56.0` brings 4 sub-step commits (`-0`/`-1`/`-2`/
`-3`) back to `main`; `git log --first-parent main` shows
one jump from `0.55.0` → `0.56.0`, the sub-steps stay
reachable via the merge's second parent.

content.content:
915	  sub-sub retired; one cycle protocol with three phases:
916	  **Preparation / Work-N / Close-out**. The `.`-separator
917	  nested numbering, the trailing-`0`-marks-Preparation
918	  rule, and the ordering caveat all live in
919	  `### Numbering`.
920	- **Push and squash are discretionary** — actions taken
921	  when wanted (back up, publish, tidy), not modes chosen
922	  at cycle start; the close-out push is mandatory; the
923	  **squash / merge (non-FF) / keep** shape decision lives
924	  at push time. The non-FF merge ("trapezoid": cycle's
925	  sub-step commits stay reachable as a branch; main reads
926	  linearly via `--first-parent`) is added as a third shape
927	  this cycle.
928	- **`.claude` once per push** — the bot repo accumulates
929	  session data across the cycle's commits and commits once
930	  per push. The per-sub-step `.claude` cadence is gone;
931	  with push decoupled from the commit cadence, `.claude`
932	  simply follows the push, and an N:1 push (no squash)
933	  emits a multi-ochid list in the `.claude` commit.
934	- **Two-gate review model** — review happens **before**
935	  each commit: **Gate 1** (work — review the working-copy
936	  changes in your editor), then write the description,
937	  then **Gate 2** (message), then commit. ESC-ESC and
938	  "stop on deviation/question" override. The earlier
939	  commit-first model (review the committed revision) is
940	  gone.
941	- **CLAUDE.md consolidated** — the scattered cycle /
942	  commit / push material collapses into one linear
943	  `## Cycle Protocol` that reads top-to-bottom as the flow
944	  (intro → `### Numbering` → `### Preparation` → `###
945	  Per-commit flow` → `### Reviewing changes` → `###
946	  Close-out` → `### Pushing` → `### ochid trailers`).
947	  `## Committing`, `## Commit Message Style`, `### User
948	  approval`, `### Versioning`, `### Pre-commit checklist`,
949	  `## ochid Trailers`, and `## Commit-Push-Finalize Flow`
950	  are gone; `vc-x1 push` flag/stage/recovery detail
951	  deferred to `vc-x1 push --help`. CLAUDE.md shrinks ~39%
952	  (1035 → 632 lines).
953	
954	### As-built ladder

file.content:
sub-sub retired; one cycle protocol with three phases:
  **Preparation / Work-N / Close-out**. The `.`-separator
  nested numbering, the trailing-`0`-marks-Preparation
  rule, and the ordering caveat all live in
  `### Numbering`.
- **Push and squash are discretionary** — actions taken
  when wanted (back up, publish, tidy), not modes chosen
  at cycle start; the close-out push is mandatory; the
  **squash / merge (non-FF) / keep** shape decision lives
  at push time. The non-FF merge ("trapezoid": cycle's
  sub-step commits stay reachable as a branch; main reads
  linearly via `--first-parent`) is added as a third shape
  this cycle.
- **`.claude` once per push** — the bot repo accumulates
  session data across the cycle's commits and commits once
  per push. The per-sub-step `.claude` cadence is gone;
  with push decoupled from the commit cadence, `.claude`
  simply follows the push, and an N:1 push (no squash)
  emits a multi-ochid list in the `.claude` commit.
- **Two-gate review model** — review happens **before**
  each commit: **Gate 1** (work — review the working-copy
  changes in your editor), then write the description,
  then **Gate 2** (message), then commit. ESC-ESC and
  "stop on deviation/question" override. The earlier
  commit-first model (review the committed revision) is
  gone.
- **CLAUDE.md consolidated** — the scattered cycle /
  commit / push material collapses into one linear
  `## Cycle Protocol` that reads top-to-bottom as the flow
  (intro → `### Numbering` → `### Preparation` → `###
  Per-commit flow` → `### Reviewing changes` → `###
  Close-out` → `### Pushing` → `### ochid trailers`).
  `## Committing`, `## Commit Message Style`, `### User
  approval`, `### Versioning`, `### Pre-commit checklist`,
  `## ochid Trailers`, and `## Commit-Push-Finalize Flow`
  are gone; `vc-x1 push` flag/stage/recovery detail
  deferred to `vc-x1 push --help`. CLAUDE.md shrinks ~39%
  (1035 → 632 lines).

### As-built ladder

content.text: The 0.56.0 outcome subsection in chores-11.md already documents the trapezoid shape, so I can reference the existing `[32]` link. Inserting the new todo right after #25 (the `--squash` design entry), since both are close-out shape choices at push time.

input.old_string:
25. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
    place first. [[12]]
26. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
input.new_string:
25. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
    `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
    `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
    place first. [[12]]
26. vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape). Set up the
    non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape automatically —
    `jj rebase -r <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>` so `main`
    gains one merge commit with two parents (previous tip +
    cycle sub-tip) — instead of requiring the user to run
    that rebase manually before `vc-x1 push`. Sibling to
    the `--squash` entry above; both are close-out shape
    choices made at push time. Dogfooded manually in
    `0.56.0`. [[32]]
26. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit

attachment.snippet:
1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
19	
20	_No cycle currently in progress._
21	
22	## Todo
23	
24	 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
25	 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
26	 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
27	 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
28	
29	 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so
30	 you can grep, count, and reference them ("let's work on
31	 #3"). You don't hand-maintain the numbers — insert, delete,
32	 or reorder entries freely, then `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run`
33	 renumbers the list and normalizes continuation-line indent.
34	 `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo`
35	 is the read-only check.
36	
37	1. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Set up
38	   the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape automatically —
39	   `jj rebase -r <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>` so `main`
40	   gains one merge commit with two parents (previous tip +
41	   cycle sub-tip) — instead of requiring the user to run
42	   that rebase manually before `vc-x1 push`. Sibling to
43	   the `--squash` entry below (#25); both are close-out
44	   shape choices made at push time. Dogfooded manually in
45	   `0.56.0`. [[32]]
46	2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
47	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
48	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
49	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
50	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
51	   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
52	   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
53	   (`init.rs`, tests).
54	
55	   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
56	   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
57	   issue.
58	3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
59	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
60	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
61	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
62	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
63	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
64	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
65	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
66	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
67	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
68	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
69	4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
70	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
71	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
72	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
73	   the felt bottleneck.
74	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
75	5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
76	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
77	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
78	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
79	   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
80	   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
81	6. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
82	   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
83	   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
84	   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
85	   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
86	   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
87	   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
88	   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
89	7. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
90	   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
91	   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
92	   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
93	   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
94	   questions in the stub. [[4]]
95	8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
96	   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
97	   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
98	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
99	   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
100	   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
101	   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
102	   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
103	   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
104	   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
105	   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
106	9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
107	   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
108	   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
109	   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
110	   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
111	   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
112	   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
113	   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
114	   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
115	10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
116	    Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
117	    → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
118	    signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
119	    communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
120	11. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
121	    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
122	    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
123	    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
124	    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
125	    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
126	    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
127	12. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
128	    `chores-08.md` (the 0.41.1 cycle) landed on `main` via
129	    the `0.42.0-4.7` rebase; check that section ordering
130	    across `chores-06`/`-07`/`-08`/`-09` is chronologically
131	    coherent and normalize if not. Low priority.
132	13. Add a vc-x1 validate-repo?
133	14. vc-x1 push: rework the two bookmark parameters.
134	    `PushArgs` has `bookmark_pos` (positional `BOOKMARK`) +
135	    `bookmark` (`--bookmark` flag) for one logical value,
136	    forcing an `or_else` in `From<&PushArgs>`. Collapse to a
137	    single positional with `--bookmark` as a true clap alias,
138	    or drop one spelling. [[10]]
139	15. vc-x1 push: `--recheck` — implement or remove. Parsed by
140	    `PushArgs`, never read; mirrored into `PushParams` with

148	    [[12]],[[13]],[[14]],[[15]]
149	17. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag.
150	    Parallel to `init --scope` for role selection;

154	18. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:

input.new_string:
1. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Teach
   push to set up the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape
   itself instead of requiring the user to pre-rebase
   before invoking push. Sibling to `--squash` (#26);
   both are close-out shape choices made at push time.
   Dogfooded manually in `0.56.0`. [[32]]
   - Preparation (`-0`): open chores section; settle flag
     surface (`--merge` mutually exclusive with `--squash`
     / `--keep`?) and preflight checks (cycle tip is
     descendant of `<prev>`, not already a merge, sub-tip
     reachable from current bookmark, etc.).
   - Work-1 (`-1`): parse the flag through `PushArgs` →
     `PushParams`; reject mutually-exclusive combinations.
   - Work-2 (`-2`): implement the rebase setup inside
     push's state machine — locate `<tip>` and
     `<sub-tip>`, run the equivalent of `jj rebase -r
     <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>`; ensure the `.claude`
     commit emits a multi-line `ochid:` covering every
     sub-step in the merge.
   - Work-3 (`-3`): tests + dogfood on a real cycle.
   - Close-out: finalize chores; `## Done` entry; CLAUDE.md
     `### Pushing` text updated to describe the flag.

## docs: refine cycle protocol (0.56.0)

Refines CLAUDE.md's cycle protocol end-to-end. Picked up
from `## Todo` item 1 (codify the move-into-`## In
Progress` workflow); grew through the cycle into a broader
rework:

  its text into `## In Progress` so the section is
  self-describing; the chores opener **mirrors** that
  content so the cycle stays readable once `## In Progress`
  is cleared at close-out; the `## Done` one-liner with a
  chores `[N]` ref is added at close-out.
- **One protocol** — single-step / multi-step / sub-step /
  sub-sub retired; one cycle protocol with three phases:

868	print an identical per-entry report line.

873	- 0.55.0-1 add validate-todo subcommand + todo_helpers

882	planned.

884	The one reversal was the opener's workflow experiment.
885	`0.55.0-0`'s `### Keeping the active task in ## Todo`
886	switched CLAUDE.md from delete-on-pickup to a keep-#1 model
887	(the picked-up `## Todo` entry stays under a `### Current In
888	Progress` heading, linked from `## In Progress`). It did its
889	job — the active task stayed visible in `## Todo` for easy
890	dogfooding mid-cycle — but at close-out the user judged it
891	not worth keeping. This commit reverts CLAUDE.md to plain

895	`## Todo` item for a later cycle.

898	`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` — the cycle's own tool on its
899	own backlog, and the close-out dogfood.

file.content:
## feat: validate-todo + fix-todo (0.55.0)

Commits: [[16]]

Cycle close-out. The `validate-todo` / `fix-todo` subcommand
pair — a Rust replacement for `notes/fix-todo.py` — is in:
`validate-todo` checks `## Todo` / `## Bugs` numbering and
continuation indent, `fix-todo` renumbers and re-indents
(dry-run by default). Both share the `todo_helpers` core and
print an identical per-entry report line.

### As-built ladder

- 0.55.0-0 plan + version bump + chores opener
- 0.55.0-1 add validate-todo subcommand + todo_helpers
- 0.55.0-2 add fix-todo subcommand; docs sweep; delete
  notes/fix-todo.py
- 0.55.0 close-out

### Outcome

Two subcommands and a docs sweep over three steps, no
evaluation-gate detours — the subcommand work landed as
planned.

The one reversal was the opener's workflow experiment.
`0.55.0-0`'s `### Keeping the active task in ## Todo`
switched CLAUDE.md from delete-on-pickup to a keep-#1 model
(the picked-up `## Todo` entry stays under a `### Current In
Progress` heading, linked from `## In Progress`). It did its
job — the active task stayed visible in `## Todo` for easy
dogfooding mid-cycle — but at close-out the user judged it
not worth keeping. This commit reverts CLAUDE.md to plain
delete-on-pickup. A refined idea — move the picked-up entry
*into* `## In Progress` (self-describing, no heading hack)
and capture it in the chores opener — is queued as a
`## Todo` item for a later cycle.

Dropping `## Todo` item 1 and renumbering was done with
`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run` — the cycle's own tool on its
own backlog, and the close-out dogfood.

content.text:
Here's the plan I'd execute — a 3-commit cycle (`-0`, `-1`, close-out), squashed before push:

**Preparation `0.57.0-0`**
- `jj new --insert-before @` — inserts an empty commit between `0.56.0` and the current @ (todo.md edits). Working copy lands on Preparation; the existing change (chid `xnspotmrlzqo`) rebases as a child and becomes Work-1.
- Backfill `0.56.0`'s `Commits:` ref — add `[17]` → `25dfcb8edfba` (`25dfcb8edfba7ed81b0a51dd971cdc2e3a371f0d`) in `chores-11.md`'s References; insert `Commits: [[17]]` under the section header at line 902.
- Open a new chores section in `chores-11.md` (provisional header `## docs: add --merge todo entry (0.57.0-0)` + intro mirroring `## In Progress`).
- Replace `_No cycle currently in progress._` in `notes/todo.md > ## In Progress` with overview + ladder (`-0 (current)`, `-1`, close-out). Mark `-0 (current)` first, do the work, flip to `(done)` before commit.
- Bump `Cargo.toml` to `0.57.0-0`.
- Cargo fmt/clippy/test/install.
- Gate 1 → Gate 2 → commit.

**Work-1 `0.57.0-1`**
- Move @ to the rebased child (todo.md entry #1 already there).
- Flip ladder: `-1 (current)`, then `-1 (done)`.
- Bump `Cargo.toml` to `0.57.0-1`. Cargo cycle. Gates → commit.

**Close-out `0.57.0`**
- Clear `## In Progress` back to `_No cycle currently in progress._`; add one-liner to `## Done` with chores ref.
- Sync chores header to final commit title; update todo.md `## Done` anchor + chores anchor; add `### As-built ladder`.
- Bump `Cargo.toml` to `0.57.0`. Cargo cycle. Gates → commit.

**Squash & push**
- `jj squash` -0 and -1 into close-out (close-out's title wins). Chores header already matches; no anchor churn.
- `vc-x1 push main`.

Two questions before I start:

options.label: Just the merge commit (Recommended)
options.description: `Commits: [[17]]` pointing at 25dfcb8edfba. Sub-steps are reachable via the merge's second parent + `git log --grep`. Matches the singular phrasing in CLAUDE.md's `Timing` paragraph.

options.label: All 5 commits
options.description: `Commits: [[17]],[[18]],[[19]],[[20]],[[21]]` — merge + 4 sub-steps. Full citability per-step from the chores section. Adds 5 refs to chores-11.

questions.question: Run all three commits to completion before stopping, or stop at each Gate 1?

options.label: Stop at every Gate (Recommended)
options.description: Strict protocol — work-review (Gate 1) + message-review (Gate 2) before each of the 3 commits. 6 stops total.

options.label: Stop at Gates for Preparation only
options.description: Preparation is the structural step; Work-1 is just the existing edits + marker flip; Close-out is housekeeping. Treat the whole cycle as one review unit — stop only at the squashed-final Gates before push.

50	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself

53	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1

Landed via the new **non-FF merge** ("trapezoid") shape —
dogfooded for the first time this cycle. The merge commit
at bare `0.56.0` brings 4 sub-step commits (`-0`/`-1`/`-2`/
`-3`) back to `main`; `git log --first-parent main` shows
one jump from `0.55.0` → `0.56.0`, the sub-steps stay
reachable via the merge's second parent.

## docs: add --merge todo entry (0.57.0-0)

Adds a `## Todo` entry recording a future `vc-x1 push:
--merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to the planned
`--squash`) that automates setting up the non-FF merge
("trapezoid") shape so users don't have to pre-rebase
before `vc-x1 push`. The shape was dogfooded manually in
`0.56.0`; this cycle captures the design as a todo and
walks the Preparation → Work-N → Close-out protocol on a
deliberately small change — the cycle's deliverable is one
new todo entry, squashed at close-out and pushed.

# References

3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11

13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

22	`--squash`) that automates setting up the non-FF merge

26	walks the Preparation → Work-N → Close-out protocol on a
27	deliberately small change — the cycle's deliverable is one
28	new todo entry, squashed at close-out and pushed.
29	
30	   - 0.57.0-0 Preparation: backfill `0.56.0` `Commits:`
31	     ref; open chores section; set up `## In Progress`.
32	     (done)
33	   - 0.57.0-1 Work-1: add the `--merge` entry to `## Todo`
34	     with intro + Preparation/Work-N/Close-out ladder.
35	     (current)
36	   - 0.57.0 Close-out: clear `## In Progress`; add `##
37	     Done` one-liner; finalize chores section; squash and
38	     push.
39	
40	## Todo
41	
42	 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
43	 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
44	 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
45	 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
46	
47	 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so
48	 you can grep, count, and reference them ("let's work on
49	 #3"). You don't hand-maintain the numbers — insert, delete,
50	 or reorder entries freely, then `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run`
51	 renumbers the list and normalizes continuation-line indent.
52	 `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo`
53	 is the read-only check.
54	
55	1. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Teach
56	   push to set up the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape
57	   itself instead of requiring the user to pre-rebase
58	   before invoking push. Sibling to `--squash` (#26);
59	   both are close-out shape choices made at push time.
60	   Dogfooded manually in `0.56.0`. [[32]]
61	   - Preparation (`-0`): open chores section; settle flag
62	     surface (`--merge` mutually exclusive with `--squash`
63	     / `--keep`?) and preflight checks (cycle tip is
64	     descendant of `<prev>`, not already a merge, sub-tip
65	     reachable from current bookmark, etc.).
66	   - Work-1 (`-1`): parse the flag through `PushArgs` →
67	     `PushParams`; reject mutually-exclusive combinations.
68	   - Work-2 (`-2`): implement the rebase setup inside
69	     push's state machine — locate `<tip>` and
70	     `<sub-tip>`, run the equivalent of `jj rebase -r
71	     <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>`; ensure the `.claude`
72	     commit emits a multi-line `ochid:` covering every
73	     sub-step in the merge.
74	   - Work-3 (`-3`): tests + dogfood on a real cycle.
75	   - Close-out: finalize chores; `## Done` entry; CLAUDE.md
76	     `### Pushing` text updated to describe the flag.
77	2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
78	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
79	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
80	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
81	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
82	   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
83	   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
84	   (`init.rs`, tests).
85	
86	   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
87	   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
88	   issue.
89	3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
90	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
91	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
92	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
93	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
94	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
95	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
96	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
97	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
98	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
99	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
100	4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
101	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
102	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
103	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
104	   the felt bottleneck.
105	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
106	5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
107	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
108	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
109	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
110	   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
111	   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
112	6. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
113	   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
114	   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
115	   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
116	   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
117	   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
118	   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
119	   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
120	7. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
121	   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
122	   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
123	   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
124	   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
125	   questions in the stub. [[4]]
126	8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
127	   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
128	   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
129	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
130	   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
131	   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
132	   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
133	   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
134	   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
135	   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
136	   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
137	9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
138	   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
139	   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
140	   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
141	   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
142	   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
143	   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
144	   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
145	   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
146	10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

_No cycle currently in progress._

 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.

     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename
     on write would close this.
   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
     where there may be no next run.

   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
   patches one related case — the detached child eating
   its own prior markers before the user can see them —

problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed

     surfacing could read partial content. Atomic-rename

   - **No notify-at-failure path.** A detached
     `finalize --exec` failure only becomes visible when
     the user next runs *any* `vc-x1` command — fine for
     interactive use, invisible for CI / scheduled use
     where there may be no next run.

   The exec-child gate in `main.rs` (since 0.52.0-3)
   patches one related case — the detached child eating
   its own prior markers before the user can see them —

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
19	
20	Adds a `## Todo` entry recording a future `vc-x1 push:
21	--merge` flag (close-out shape, sibling to the planned
22	`--squash`) that automates setting up the non-FF merge
23	("trapezoid") shape so users don't have to pre-rebase
24	before `vc-x1 push`. The shape was dogfooded manually in
25	`0.56.0`; this cycle captures the design as a todo and
26	walks the Preparation → Work-N → Close-out protocol on a
27	deliberately small change — the cycle's deliverable is one
28	new todo entry, squashed at close-out and pushed.
29	
30	   - 0.57.0-0 Preparation: backfill `0.56.0` `Commits:`
31	     ref; open chores section; set up `## In Progress`.
32	     (done)
33	   - 0.57.0-1 Work-1: add the `--merge` entry to `## Todo`
34	     with intro + Preparation/Work-N/Close-out ladder.
35	     (done)
36	   - 0.57.0 Close-out: clear `## In Progress`; add `##
37	     Done` one-liner; finalize chores section; squash and
38	     push.
39	
40	## Todo
41	
42	 A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
43	 highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
44	 Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
45	 in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.
46	
47	 Entries carry explicit `1.` `2.` … numbers in the source so
48	 you can grep, count, and reference them ("let's work on
49	 #3"). You don't hand-maintain the numbers — insert, delete,
50	 or reorder entries freely, then `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run`
51	 renumbers the list and normalizes continuation-line indent.
52	 `vc-x1 fix-todo` alone only previews; `vc-x1 validate-todo`
53	 is the read-only check.
54	
55	1. **vc-x1 push: `--merge` flag (close-out shape).** Teach
56	   push to set up the non-FF merge ("trapezoid") shape
57	   itself instead of requiring the user to pre-rebase
58	   before invoking push. Sibling to `--squash` (#26);
59	   both are close-out shape choices made at push time.
60	   Dogfooded manually in `0.56.0`. [[32]]
61	   - Preparation (`-0`): open chores section; settle flag
62	     surface (`--merge` mutually exclusive with `--squash`
63	     / `--keep`?) and preflight checks (cycle tip is
64	     descendant of `<prev>`, not already a merge, sub-tip
65	     reachable from current bookmark, etc.).
66	   - Work-1 (`-1`): parse the flag through `PushArgs` →
67	     `PushParams`; reject mutually-exclusive combinations.
68	   - Work-2 (`-2`): implement the rebase setup inside
69	     push's state machine — locate `<tip>` and
70	     `<sub-tip>`, run the equivalent of `jj rebase -r
71	     <tip> -d <prev> -d <sub-tip>`; ensure the `.claude`
72	     commit emits a multi-line `ochid:` covering every
73	     sub-step in the merge.
74	   - Work-3 (`-3`): tests + dogfood on a real cycle.
75	   - Close-out: finalize chores; `## Done` entry; CLAUDE.md
76	     `### Pushing` text updated to describe the flag.
77	2. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
78	   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
79	   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
80	   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
81	   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
82	   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
83	   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
84	   (`init.rs`, tests).
85	
86	   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
87	   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
88	   issue.
89	3. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
90	   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
91	   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
92	   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
93	   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
94	   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
95	   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
96	   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
97	   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
98	   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
99	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
100	4. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
101	   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by
102	   `inventory::submit!` per subcommand. Trade-offs mirror
103	   linkme's. Pick one if/when the trait sweep's match becomes
104	   the felt bottleneck.
105	   <https://github.com/dtolnay/inventory>
106	5. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
107	   companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por
108	   workspace; emit cross-links going forward. Manual
109	   setup on an external por workspace (2026-05-14)
110	   proved arduous; this should be a routine subcommand.
111	   Design stub in [[4]] § 2.
112	6. **Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics / logging.**
113	   `vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` on `../dicom-rs`
114	   (2026-05-14) honored `--repo` throughout actual
115	   operations, but `bm-track` still emitted
116	   `.claude(main)=no-jj` — diagnostic strings have
117	   `.claude` baked in. Cosmetic today; load-bearing once
118	   bot-repo name becomes configurable (see Symmetric
119	   `.vc-config.toml` schema entry below).
120	7. **por/dual parity + `dual → por` conversion.** Make
121	   `por` and `dual` first-class equals (dual is primary
122	   today, por bolted on); add `dual → por` conversion
123	   (detach the `.claude` companion). Builds on the
124	   `--scope` rollout below. Pre-design; goal + open
125	   questions in the stub. [[4]]
126	8. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
127	   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
128	   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
129	   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
130	   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
131	   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
132	   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
133	   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
134	   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
135	   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
136	   hardens. [[5]],[[6]]
137	9. **Symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema.** Add `code = "/"`
138	   and `bot = "/.claude"` (workspace-root-relative paths) so
139	   both repos read from the same shape. Side detection walks
140	   up from cwd via existing `find_workspace_root`, then maps
141	   cwd-relative-to-root onto the configured `code` / `bot`
142	   paths. Cwd-basename match is a fast-path shortcut at
143	   role-root level only — subdirs need the walk-up. Was the
144	   deferred half of `0.41.1-6`'s title. Migration story for
145	   existing workspaces TBD at design time. [[7]]
146	10. **`test_helpers::Fixture` migration + downstream callers.**
147	    Plus rename `Fixture` → `TestFixtureDual` and `FixturePor`
148	    → `TestFixturePor` so call sites carry the test-only
149	    signal that `#[cfg(test)] mod test_helpers` doesn't
150	    communicate. Was `0.41.1-7`. [[8]]
151	11. **`vc-x1 finalize --scope` flag.** Replace `--repo`
152	    with the role vocabulary used elsewhere
153	    (`code|bot|code,bot`). Carry-over from the 0.42.0
154	    `--scope` sweep (was 0.42.0-5; deferred at -4.7
155	    close-out). The paired `Single(_)` dogfood item
156	    (0.42.0-7) is moot after `0.53.0` — `Single(_)`
157	    deleted. Design lives in chores-07. [[9]]
158	12. Cross-file `chores-NN.md` ordering sanity pass.
